'''Alex Foster''' (born 25 September 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr|lf}} or {{rlp|ce}} for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from Leeds at the [[Hunslet Hawks]], [[London Broncos]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]]. He played one season for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] before returning to the [[Super League]] with the [[Castleford Tigers]], where he spent the longest spell of his career. He appeared on dual registration from Castleford at [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]] and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. He played for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and was appointed club captain, before re-joining the [[Castleford Tigers]] on loan.

==Background==
Foster was born in [[Gloucester]], [[Gloucestershire]], England.

Foster played junior rugby league for Wetherby Bulldogs before joining the [[Leeds Rhinos]] academy system.<ref name="HA-1">{{cite web |last=White |first=Ed |date=2014-01-14 |title=Leeds Rhinos' Alex Foster joins London Broncos on loan |url=http://www.harrogateadvertiser.co.uk/sport/rugby/leeds-rhinos-alex-foster-joins-london-broncos-on-loan-1-6372854 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713201641/https://www.harrogateadvertiser.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-alex-foster-joins-london-broncos-on-loan-1-6372854 |archive-date=2018-07-13 |accessdate=29 January 2016 |website=Harrogate Advertiser}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
On 30 June 2013, Foster made his [[Super League]] début for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] against the [[Widnes Vikings]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-06-30 |title=Super League: Widnes Vikings 36-52 Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23034393 |access-date=2023-07-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref name="Leeds deal">{{Cite web |date=2013-11-01 |title=Super League: Alex Foster has signed a three-year deal with the Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/9001608/super-league-alex-foster-has-signed-a-three-year-deal-with-the-leeds-rhinos |access-date=2023-07-26 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He scored his first Super League try against the [[London Broncos]] on 1 August, and made a total 8 appearances throughout the season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-08-01 |title=Super League: London Broncos 18-30 Leeds Rhinos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23501450 |access-date=2023-07-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In November 2013, he signed a three-year deal with Leeds.<ref name="Leeds deal" /><ref name="loverugbyleague">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_13704-foster-rewarded-with-rhinos-deal.html|title=Foster rewarded with Rhinos deal|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=2014-05-28}}</ref>

==== Hunslet Hawks (dual registration) ====
In July 2013, Foster played one game for the [[Hunslet Hawks]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on dual registration from Leeds.<ref name=":4">{{Cite web |title=Alex Foster player summary |url=https://stats.rugbyleaguerecords.com/playersummary.php?tselect=1671 |access-date=2023-07-26 |website=Rugby League Record Keepers' Club}}</ref>

==== London Broncos (loan) ====
In January 2014, Foster signed a season-long loan deal with the [[London Broncos]] alongside fellow Rhinos youngsters [[Thomas Minns]] and [[James Duckworth (rugby league)|James Duckworth]].<ref name="HA-1" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-01-14 |title=London Broncos have completed the signing of Leeds Rhinos trio Alex Foster, Thomas Minns and James Duckworth |url=https://www.harrowtimes.co.uk/sport/10934660.london-broncos-have-completed-the-signing-of-leeds-rhinos-trio-alex-foster-thomas-minns-and-james-duckworth/ |access-date=2023-07-26 |website=Harrow Times}}</ref> During the season, he played as a {{Rlp|sr}} or {{Rlp|lf}} alongside [[Matt Cook (rugby league)|Matt Cook]] and [[Mike McMeeken]].<ref name=":4" /> Foster scored his first try for the Broncos against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on 10 May.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-05-10 |title=Super League: London Broncos 16-30 Huddersfield Giants |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27301654 |access-date=2023-07-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 25 May, Foster scored two tries against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in a 48–16 defeat at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-05-25 |title=Super League: Hull KR 48-16 London Broncos |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27492042 |access-date=2023-07-26 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref name="therfl">{{cite web|url=http://therfl.co.uk/report?9995|title=The RFL.|publisher=therfl.co.uk|accessdate=2014-05-28|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140529051454/http://therfl.co.uk/report?9995|archive-date=29 May 2014|url-status=dead}}</ref> He made 21 appearances in total, while London were relegated to the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==== Featherstone Rovers (loan) ====
In November 2014, [[Featherstone Rovers]] announced the signing of Foster on a season-long loan for 2015, alongside three other young Leeds players.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-12-05 |title=Featherstone Rovers chance to boost Alex Foster's hopes of Leeds Rhinos breakthrough |url=https://www.harrogateadvertiser.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-chance-to-boost-alex-fosters-hopes-of-leeds-rhinos-breakthrough-2494830 |access-date=2023-07-27 |website=Harrogate Advertiser}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-11-23 |title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos' Foster, Tonks, Baldwinson and Minns seal loan moves to Featherstone Rovers |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/9576366/super-league-leeds-rhinos-foster-tonks-baldwinson-and-minns-seal-loan-moves-to-featherstone |access-date=2023-07-27 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
[[File:Alex Foster London Broncos.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Foster warming up for the London Broncos in 2016]]
After a spell with [[Featherstone Rovers]], Foster signed for London on a two-year deal.{{citation needed|date=May 2017}} Injury meant that he was restricted to 20 appearances for the Broncos.{{citation needed|date=May 2017}}

===Bradford Bulls===
In 2017, Foster agreed a deal with Bradford however the club went into liquidation and Foster was released.<ref name=":0" />

===Castleford Tigers===
In February 2017, Castleford head coach [[Daryl Powell]] confirmed that Foster had been offered a trial with the view of a two-year deal at the end of the trial.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-bring-in-former-leeds-rhinos-and-bradford-bulls-player-alex-foster-on-trial-1-8393349|title=Castleford Tigers bring in former Leeds Rhinos and Bradford Bulls player Alex Foster on trial|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2019-06-27}}</ref> In April, it was confirmed Foster signed a deal until the end of the season with Cas and was given the number 34 shirt.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/foster-earns-permanent-castleford-deal/|title=Foster earns permanent Castleford deal|last=Gordon|first=James|date=2017-04-05|website=Love Rugby League|language=en-GB|access-date=2019-06-27}}</ref> In July, it was confirmed that Foster had signed a new deal.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|date=2017-06-09|title=Alex Foster: Castleford Tigers back row extends deal until end of 2019|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40225610|access-date=2022-02-19|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On signing the new deal, he said, "I'm over the moon to sign here for another two years. It's a great place to be."<ref name=":1" /> Foster was a replacement for Cas in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final|2017 Grand Final]] and scored their only try of the game, becoming the first Castleford player to score in a Super League Grand Final.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Cartwright|first=Phil|date=2017-10-07|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41536394|access-date=2019-06-27|website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Foster demonstrated his positional versatility in 2018, starting at loose forward, second row and centre at different points throughout the season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/alex-foster/summary.html|title=Alex Foster - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=2019-06-27}}</ref> He was rewarded for his impressive form in June with a new three-and-a-half-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5986|title=Foster lands long-term deal|website=Castleford Tigers|language=en|access-date=2019-06-27}}</ref> However, in July, Foster was ruled out for the remainder of the season due to a foot injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-tigers-foster-blow-softened-by-injury-boost-elsewhere-1-9233255|title=Castleford Tigers' Foster blow softened by injury boost elsewhere|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk|language=en|access-date=2019-06-27}}</ref>

In the 2019 season, Foster made six appearances and scored one try. His playtime was limited by a knee injury sustained in March, for which he underwent surgery later in the year.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|date=2019-06-05|title=Castleford utility Alex Foster ruled out for the season|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-utility-alex-foster-ruled-out-for-the-season/|access-date=2022-02-19|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> 
[[File:Alex Foster Castleford.jpg|thumb|right|Foster pre-game for the Castleford Tigers in 2019]]
Foster made his return to the team following the 2020 season's [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom|COVID-19]] suspension, after spending 16 months on the sidelines.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Shaw|first=Matthew|date=2020-03-29|title=Alex Foster gives insight into the painstaking boredom of rehab|url=https://www.totalrl.com/alex-foster-gives-insight-into-the-painstaking-boredom-of-rehab/|access-date=2022-02-19|website=TotalRL}}</ref> In the remainder of the season, he made 8 appearances and scored 1 try.

Foster sustained a bicep injury in a pre-season friendly against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] in March 2021.<ref name=":2">{{Cite web|last=Craven|first=Dave|date=2021-06-07|title=Frustration ends as Castleford's Alex Foster eyes Wembley|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/frustration-ends-as-castlefords-alex-foster-eyes-wembley-3264150|access-date=2022-02-19|website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> He made his first appearance of the year in Castleford's [[Challenge Cup]] semi-final against Warrington on 5 June.<ref name=":2" /> On 17 July 2021, he played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against St. Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|work=BBC Sport |date=15 July 2021 }}</ref> In September, it was announced that he would leave the club at the end of the 2021 season upon the expiry of his contract.<ref name=":3">{{Cite web|date=2021-09-13|title=Alex Foster to leave the Fords|url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7631#|access-date=2022-02-19|website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Speaking about his time at Castleford, Foster said, "It has been one hell of a journey and I am so thankful to everyone involved, from the coaching staff to the fans, my teammates as well who have made my time here what it has been for me."<ref name=":3" />

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 17 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|last=Gordon|first=James|date=2021-10-17|title=Super League forward confirms Newcastle move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-forward-alex-foster-confirms-newcastle-move/|access-date=2021-12-26|website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2021-10-17|title=Newcastle Thunder confirm Alex Foster signing|url=https://www.thunderrugby.co.uk/article/179/newcastle-thunder-confirm-alex-foster-signing|access-date=2022-02-19|website=Newcastle Thunder}}</ref> He made his first appearance and scored his first try for the club against [[Workington Town|Workington]] on 30 January.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2022-01-30|title=Workington vs Newcastle Thunder|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/workington-vs-newcastle/55869|access-date=2022-02-19|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
In July 2023, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the return of Foster, signing on a season-long loan from Newcastle, alongside [[Greece national rugby league team|Greece]] international [[Billy Tsikrikas]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=20 July 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers make double signing as ex-Leeds Rhinos man returns |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-make-double-signing-as-ex-leeds-rhinos-man-returns-4225778 |access-date=26 July 2023 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=20 July 2023 |title=Duo join the Tigers on loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8685 |access-date=26 July 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 14 November 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=14 Nov 2023|title=Sheffield Eagles swoop to sign former Super League Grand Finalist in coup for Mark Aston's side|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sheffield-eagles-swoop-to-sign-former-super-league-grand-finalist-alex-foster-for-2024}}</ref>
